items.title: As work modernises in Australia, some rare trades face decline

children.content: A shortage of locksmiths in Australia persists despite government-funded incentives. The nation's changing labour landscape has meant some specialist trades are not being chosen by apprentices.

image.altText: a man in a navy shirt outside a white trade van on a street

items.title: Ex-Liberal strategist, pollster Stansfield in Senate tilt

children.content: A former Liberal adviser turned Tasmanian political commentator says he is "genuinely worried about the future of the country" and plans to stand for preselection for the Senate at the next federal election.

image.altText: Brad Stansfield smiles at the camera.

items.title: Smelter left in limbo after consortium member effectively pulls out of bid

children.content: Workers at Tasmania's troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter have been told administrators have until Monday to secure alternative funding, after a member of the preferred consortium bidding to take over the manganese smelter effectively pulled out.

items.title: Hip-thrusting, flannel-wearing drag kings say Hobart's embraced them

children.content: Hobart's drag kings say the closure of a gay bar pushed their performances into heteronormative spaces and a surprisingly accepting Tasmanian community.

image.altText: A drag king winking in front of a crowd

items.title: TassieCast: Is this really Tasmania's 'most transparent' government?

children.content: Transparency and trust in government are cornerstones of any successful democracy. So how does it feel not knowing why almost half a million dollars of taxpayers money has so far been spent on government MPs' legal bills? Airlie Ward talks with state political reporter Josh Duggan.

items.title: Greens say premier's answers about legal fees 'paint a far worse picture'

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has provided answers to a series of questions about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fees matter, but the Greens say they "paint a far worse picture of this shameful saga and his role in it".

articles.title: What happens next for Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fee saga?

children.content: It's been two weeks since Madeleine Ogilvie resigned from Tasmanian cabinet, and the saga is reaching a tipping point. What could happen when parliament returns?

image.altText: Tasmanian parliament building from outside.

articles.title: 'A footballer's dream': From humble beginnings to the world stage

children.content: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

articles.title: Monterrey Stadium one of the most stunning World Cup venues in history

items.title: How Israel's operation 'Eternal Darkness' in southern Lebanon is expanding

items.title: The sword that links feared pirates to Britain's greatest naval hero

items.title: Feral sambar deer likely brought to SA for hunting purposes

children.content: One of Australia's largest feral deer species was spotted in SA's Riverland for the first time earlier this month. While its existence is being investigated, the Invasive Species Council says it was likely transported across state lines for hunting purposes.

items.title: What Australia would have looked like when the dinosaurs roamed
